test.summary: All tests passed across workspace. Notable: 1000 tests in swell-orchestrator, 490 in swell-tools, 380 in swell-core, 102 in swell-llm, 35 in swell-state, 18 in swell-memory, 13 in swell-validation, 12 integration tests

blockingIssues.description: Missing concurrent access verification tests. The feature's verificationSteps explicitly required 'cargo test -p swell-orchestrator --test concurrent_registry' to test concurrent reads from multiple threads and concurrent writes to different tasks. No such test exists in the codebase. The implementation correctly uses DashMap<Uuid, Arc<RwLock<Task>>> for fine-grained locking, but without the explicitly required test, we cannot verify the expected behavior of concurrent reads/writes without contention.

nonBlockingIssues.description: In complete_task(), sm.with_task_mut() returned result is discarded with 'let _ = sm.with_task_mut(...)'. Intentional but non-obvious.
After sm.with_task_mut() (which holds a read lock), we drop(sm) and re-acquire write lock. Involves two separate lock acquisitions.
Handoff claims 12 tests were added but only 11 unique test names exist. Duplicate test name 'test_task_spec_valid_custom_commit_policy'.
Missing test for invalid serde deserialization of commit_policy variants (VAL-TASK-002 contract requires this).
Team struct uses std::sync::RwLock instead of tokio::RwLock - minor inconsistency with crate's async patterns.
create_team() doc comment shows 'async fn' but implementation is synchronous.
CronEvent enum is defined and exported but never emitted - dead code.
is_due() and next_scheduled() re-parse cron expression on every invocation - minor performance issue.
is_due() is public but appears to be an internal helper method.
WorkerBootError::AlreadyFinished and WorkerBootError::WorkerNotFound variants defined but never used - dead code.
BUG: previous_state is always set to Some(new_state) instead of actual previous state. Doesn't affect state recovery but is semantically misleading.
Checkpoint errors are logged with warn! and ignored - design choice acceptable given feature scope.
If rename fails after temp file is written, temp file is left behind - theoretical edge case.
All tasks use same temp file name - harmless due to rename atomicity.
Variable 'recipe' in test declared as mut but never mutated - minor unused_mut warning.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: skillName field uses 'rust-worker' but the actual skill in .factory/skills/ is named 'rust'. Workers that validate skill existence may fail to start. Should be changed to 'rust'.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviewers (task-checkpoint-wiring, task-state-file-observability) flagged this. features.json has skillName: 'rust-worker' but .factory/skills/ contains 'rust' skill.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: verificationSteps commands don't always match actual test targets. For example, task-concurrent-registry lists 'cargo test -p swell-orchestrator --test concurrent_registry' but no such test exists. task-checkpoint-wiring lists 'cargo test -p swell-state' but tests are in swell-orchestrator. These discrepancies cause confusion and may prevent workers from running correct verification.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: task-concurrent-registry verificationSteps: '--test concurrent_registry' (doesn't exist). task-checkpoint-wiring verificationSteps: '-p swell-state --test checkpoint_wiring' but actual tests are in '-p swell-orchestrator -- checkpoint_wiring'.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance that handoff test counts should be verified against actual test file before reporting. Multiple features (task-spec-enrichment, task-state-file-observability) reported incorrect test counts in handoffs, leading to incorrect expectations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: task-spec-enrichment handoff claims 12 tests, actual is 11. task-state-file-observability handoff claims 8 tests, actual is 7.

rejectedObservations.observation: CronEvent enum was defined but never integrated into any event system - pattern inconsistency with TeamRegistry
rejectedObservations.reason: This was intentionally scoped as out-of-scope per the feature description which only required CronRegistry with due_entries(). Event emission was not part of the feature contract.

rejectedObservations.observation: CheckpointingTaskStateMachine not integrated into main Orchestrator struct
rejectedObservations.reason: Feature requirement only stated checkpoints must save on transitions and state be recoverable. Integration into Orchestrator was not explicitly required.
